engineGuide.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Volvo XC60 across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The D4204T5 brought Volvo's efficient 2.0L twin-turbo diesel to the XC60, replacing older five-cylinders with improved economy but introducing new injector and timing concerns.
compatibleVariants: D4 Core, D4 Plus, D4 R-Design, D4 Inscription, D4 Momentum, D4 EfficientDynamics
commonFailures: Injector seal leak causing diesel odour and rough idle (typically 60k-90k miles)
Timing belt tensioner wear requiring preventative replacement by 108k miles
EGR cooler leak leading to coolant loss and overheating warnings
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T5 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: An updated version of the D4204T5 with revised piston rings to address oil consumption, fitted to later P3 and early P5 generation XC60 models.
compatibleVariants: D4 Core (P5), D4 Plus (P5), D4 R-Design (P5), D4 Inscription (P5)
commonFailures: High-pressure fuel pump wear causing hard starts and reduced power
AdBlue injector clogging on Euro 6 units leading to NOx system faults
Turbocharger actuator failure resulting in limp mode above 80k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T14 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Lower-output D3 variant sharing the Drive-E architecture, offering strong economy for fleet and private buyers prioritising running costs over performance.
compatibleVariants: D3 Core, D3 Momentum, D3 Inscription
commonFailures: DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles triggering regeneration faults
EGR valve carbon buildup causing rough idle and fault codes
Oil dilution from frequent regenerations on high-mileage examples
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T4 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Mild Hybrid Diesel
entries.history: The B4 mild-hybrid diesel introduced 48V technology to improve efficiency and enable coasting, fitted to P5 generation XC60 from 2020 onward.
compatibleVariants: B4 Core AWD, B4 Plus AWD, B4 Ultimate AWD
commonFailures: 48V belt-starter generator failure causing loss of hybrid assist and warning lights
Lithium-ion battery degradation reducing fuel economy gains after 70k miles
AdBlue system faults on Euro 6d units requiring software updates
entries.cta: Get quotes for D4204T23 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Volvo's legendary 2.4L five-cylinder diesel powered early XC60 models, prized for torque and refinement but requiring strict timing belt maintenance.
compatibleVariants: D5 AWD, D5 R-Design AWD, D5 Inscription AWD, D5 Momentum AWD, D5 PowerPulse AWD
commonFailures: Piston ring wear causing excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles) after 80k miles
Timing belt failure if not replaced by 108k miles or 10 years-catastrophic on interference engine
Turbocharger oil seal failure leading to blue smoke and loss of boost
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T10 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Updated D5 with revised turbocharging and emissions hardware, fitted to late P3 XC60 models with improved economy but similar failure patterns.
compatibleVariants: D5 AWD (late P3), D5 R-Design AWD (late P3), D5 Inscription AWD (late P3)
commonFailures: Swirl flap actuator failure causing inlet manifold faults and reduced power
Injector tip carbon buildup leading to poor atomisation and misfires
Oil cooler gasket leak resulting in coolant contamination
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T11 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Diesel
entries.history: Final iteration of the five-cylinder D5, featuring refined turbocharging and Euro 6 compliance before the transition to four-cylinder Drive-E units.
compatibleVariants: D4 AWD (late P3), D4 Inscription AWD (late P3)
commonFailures: EGR cooler internal leak causing coolant loss without external signs
Variable geometry turbo vanes sticking due to carbon buildup
High-pressure fuel pump wear triggering hard starts when hot
entries.cta: Get quotes for D5244T21 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: The T5 Drive-E petrol brought strong performance and reasonable economy to the XC60, with a robust bottom end but known timing chain tensioner concerns.
compatibleVariants: T5 Core, T5 Plus, T5 R-Design, T5 Inscription, T5 Momentum, Ultimate T5 AWD
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattles on cold start (typically 60k-100k miles)
Carbon buildup on intake valves leading to rough idle and reduced power
High-pressure fuel pump failure triggering limp mode and fault codes
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T19 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol Twin-Charged
entries.history: The T6 Drive-E combined turbocharging and supercharging for class-leading performance, though complexity introduced supercharger clutch failure risks.
compatibleVariants: T6 Core AWD, T6 Plus AWD, T6 R-Design AWD, T6 Inscription AWD, Ultimate T6 AWD
commonFailures: Supercharger clutch failure causing loss of low-end boost and rattling noise
Timing chain guide wear on early units leading to chain stretch and misfires
Turbocharger wastegate actuator failure resulting in overboost faults
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T29 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Mild Hybrid Petrol
entries.history: The B6 mild-hybrid petrol added 48V technology to the Drive-E architecture, improving efficiency while maintaining strong performance for P5 XC60 models.
compatibleVariants: B6 Ultimate AWD, B6 Plus AWD
commonFailures: Belt-starter generator failure causing loss of hybrid assist and warning lights
48V lithium-ion battery degradation reducing fuel economy after 60k miles
Turbocharger oil feed restriction leading to bearing wear on high-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B420T Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L Petrol
entries.history: Volvo's naturally aspirated 3.2L SI6 inline-six offered smooth, reliable power for early XC60 models, though fuel economy lagged behind newer turbo units.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals causing camshaft wear
PCV system failure leading to oil leaks and rough idle
Timing chain guide wear on high-mileage examples causing rattles
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6324S Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol Turbo
entries.history: The rare 3.0L turbocharged inline-six delivered flagship performance for early XC60 T6 models, with strong power but limited UK availability.
compatibleVariants: T6 AWD (early P3), T6 R-Design AWD (early P3)
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal failure causing blue smoke and oil consumption
High-pressure fuel pump wear triggering hard starts and reduced power
Timing chain tensioner wear on early units leading to rattles
entries.cta: Get quotes for B6304T4 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Plug-in Hybrid
entries.history: The T8 Twin Engine combined a Drive-E petrol engine with an electric rear axle for performance and efficiency, though ERAD failures became a known concern.
compatibleVariants: T8 Twin Engine Plus, T8 Twin Engine Ultimate, T8 Recharge Core, T8 Recharge Plus, T8 Recharge Ultimate, Polestar Engineered T8
commonFailures: ERAD (Electric Rear Axle Drive) bearing failure causing noise and loss of electric drive (typically 40k-80k miles)
High-voltage battery degradation reducing EV-only range after 70k miles
Charge port door fault preventing plug-in charging on early T8 units
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204T48 Volvo XC60 engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Plug-in Hybrid (Updated)
entries.history: Revised T8 Recharge powertrain with larger battery and improved thermal management, fitted to post-2021 XC60 models for extended electric range.
compatibleVariants: T8 Recharge Ultimate (2021+), T8 Recharge Polestar Engineered (2021+)
commonFailures: ERAD inverter fault causing intermittent loss of electric drive and warning lights
Onboard charger failure preventing AC charging on early 2021 units
Coolant pump failure in battery thermal system triggering reduced power mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for B4204TSH Volvo XC60 engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Volvo XC60's engine reliability varies significantly by powertrain: the D4/D5 diesels dominate UK replacement enquiries due to timing belt and injector issues, while Drive-E petrol units (T5/T6) face timing chain and supercharger concerns, and T8 hybrids require specialist attention for ERAD failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your specific XC60.

problems.h4: D4/D5 Diesel Timing Belt Failure - Why Preventative Replacement Saves Your XC60 Engine
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000-110,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps, valves collide with pistons on this interference engine, causing catastrophic damage requiring full replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative Belt Kit Replacement - Full kit with tensioners, water pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioners, idlers, water pump; reset service counter
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory spec; recommended every 108k miles or 10 years

repairOptions.tier: Post-Failure Cylinder Head Repair - Valves, guides, head skim
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace bent valves, reseat guides, machine head surface
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if block undamaged; high labour cost, limited longevity vs replacement

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit rebuilt/reconditioned long block with upgraded timing components, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage XC60; 12-month warranty; avoids hidden damage risks

problems.recommendation: If your XC60 D4/D5 is approaching 100k miles and the timing belt history is uncertain, invest in a preventative kit replacement-it's far cheaper than a snapped belt. However, if the belt has already failed or the engine has over 120k miles with oil consumption issues, a rebuilt engine with upgraded internals offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D4204T5/D5244T10 engine prices for your Volvo XC60

problems.h4: D4 Diesel Injector Seal Leak - The Smell That Signals Costly Damage Ahead
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injector copper seals harden and crack, allowing diesel to leak into the cylinder head cavity; prolonged leakage can wash oil from cylinder walls, accelerating wear.

repairOptions.tier: Injector Seal Replacement - All four injectors resealed
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove injectors, replace copper seals and washers, torque to spec, road test
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; does not address injector tip wear or internal damage

repairOptions.tier: Injector Refurbishment + Seal Kit - Refurbished injectors fitted
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove, test, refurbish or replace injectors; fit new seals; calibrate via diagnostics
repairOptions.longevity: Better longevity if injectors show wear; still leaves risk of cylinder scoring

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit rebuilt long block with upgraded piston rings and seals; bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil consumption or compression loss detected; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: A diesel smell from the engine bay on a D4 XC60 warrants immediate inspection. If compression tests are healthy and no scoring is found, seal replacement is cost-effective. But if oil consumption exceeds 1L per 1,000 miles or compression is low, a rebuilt engine prevents repeat failures and protects your investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for D4204T5 injector repair or engine replacement for your XC60

problems.h4: D5 Five-Cylinder Oil Consumption - Piston Ring Wear That Demands Action
problems.rootCause: Piston oil control rings stick or wear, allowing oil to pass into the combustion chamber; burning oil fouls DPFs and accelerates turbocharger wear if unchecked.

repairOptions.tier: PCV System Refresh + Oil Change - Address secondary causes
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace PCV valve, breather hoses; flush crankcase; use high-quality synthetic oil
repairOptions.longevity: May reduce consumption if PCV fault is primary cause; rarely resolves ring wear

repairOptions.tier: Piston Ring Replacement (Engine Out) - Major mechanical repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, strip bottom end, replace rings, reassemble; extensive labour
repairOptions.longevity: Technically sound but labour-intensive; rebuilt engine often better value with warranty

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded rings
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit rebuilt long block with upgraded piston rings and bearings; bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Best solution for high-mileage D5s; eliminates oil consumption; 12-month warranty included

problems.recommendation: If your XC60 D5 is burning over 1L of oil per 1,000 miles and has over 90k miles, ring replacement is rarely cost-effective versus a rebuilt unit. The rebuilt engine option includes upgraded rings designed to resist sticking, plus warranty protection-critical for an ageing five-cylinder.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt D5244T10 engine prices for your Volvo XC60 D5

problems.h4: T6 Twin-Charged Supercharger Clutch Failure - Performance Loss With a Price Tag
problems.rootCause: The electromagnetic clutch that engages the supercharger wears or fails electrically; loss of low-end boost reduces drivability and triggers fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove supercharger, replace clutch assembly, recalibrate; test boost pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if clutch is sole fault; does not address turbo or timing chain wear

repairOptions.tier: Supercharger Unit Replacement - Complete assembly swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned or new supercharger unit; update software; road test
repairOptions.longevity: Better if housing or bearings are worn; still leaves engine wear risks

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit rebuilt long block with upgraded timing components; bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has over 70k miles or shows timing chain noise; comprehensive solution

problems.recommendation: Supercharger clutch failure on a T6 XC60 is expensive but often isolated. If the engine is under 60k miles with no timing chain noise, clutch replacement is sensible. For higher mileage or if chain rattles are present, a rebuilt engine with upgraded timing components offers better long-term security.
problems.cta: Get quotes for B4204T29 supercharger repair or engine replacement for your XC60

problems.h4: T8 Twin Engine ERAD Failure - When the Electric Rear Axle Stops Working
problems.rootCause: The ERAD (Electric Rear Axle Drive) motor bearings wear prematurely due to thermal cycling and moisture ingress; failure causes noise, loss of electric drive, and warning lights.

repairOptions.tier: ERAD Bearing Replacement - Specialist hybrid repair
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove ERAD unit, replace bearings and seals, refill coolant; recalibrate
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if motor windings intact; requires hybrid-certified technician

repairOptions.tier: ERAD Unit Replacement - Complete rear motor swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit reconditioned ERAD assembly; update high-voltage system software; test
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if motor shows electrical faults; still leaves petrol engine wear risks

repairOptions.tier: Full Powertrain Replacement - Rebuilt petrol engine + ERAD inspection
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit rebuilt Drive-E petrol engine; inspect/test ERAD; full hybrid system calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage T8s or if petrol engine shows wear; comprehensive warranty coverage

problems.recommendation: ERAD failure on a T8 XC60 requires specialist hybrid expertise. If the petrol engine is healthy and under 60k miles, ERAD bearing replacement may suffice. However, if the petrol engine shows timing chain noise or oil consumption, a full powertrain rebuild with upgraded components offers the most secure long-term solution.
problems.cta: Enquire about B4204T48 T8 Recharge powertrain replacement for your XC60

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Volvo XC60
finalCta.paragraph: Whether your XC60 suffers from D4 injector leaks, D5 oil consumption, T6 supercharger faults, or T8 ERAD failure, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Volvo XC60 specialists. Every rebuilt or reconditioned engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components designed to address known failure points, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Volvo XC60 Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Volvo XC60 and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Volvo XC60 engine type is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges based on variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Volvo XC60 Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Professionally stripped, cleaned and rebuilt with new wear parts; ideal for XC60 owners seeking OEM-spec reliability without new-engine cost.
types.frontDescription: Professionally stripped, cleaned and rebuilt with new wear parts; ideal for XC60 owners seeking OEM-spec reliability without new-engine cost.
types.frontDisclaimer: Prices vary by engine code, mileage and supplier location.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned XC60 engines undergo full disassembly, machining and replacement of pistons, bearings and seals. Common codes like D4204T5 and B4204T19 are widely supported.
backBullets: Includes upgraded timing components to address known failure points
Best for XC60s under 120k miles with no block damage

types.title: Volvo XC60 Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Comprehensive rebuild with precision machining and OEM-spec parts; the balanced choice for XC60 owners prioritising longevity and warranty protection.
types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild with precision machining and OEM-spec parts; the balanced choice for XC60 owners prioritising longevity and warranty protection.

types.backDescription: Rebuilt XC60 engines feature cylinder honing, new rings, bearings and gaskets, plus updated components for known weaknesses like D5 piston rings or T6 supercharger clutches.
backBullets: Upgraded internals address model-specific failure patterns
Full dyno testing; 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Recommended for high-mileage XC60s or post-failure replacement

backBullets: Verify mileage, service records and absence of oil consumption
Shorter warranty (3-6 months typical); inspect before fitting
Suitable for budget-conscious XC60 owners with trusted fitters

Extended warranty options (24 months); hybrid-certified for T8
Best for late-model XC60s, performance trims or fleet operators

Shorter lead time than full rebuild; 6-12 month warranty typical

engineSizes.h2: Volvo XC60 Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Volvo XC60 is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Drive-E diesel dominates UK XC60 replacement enquiries, powering D3 and D4 variants from 2014 onward with strong economy but requiring strict timing belt maintenance on pre-2016 units.
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC60 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.4L Diesel Engines
items.description: The legendary 2.4L five-cylinder diesel powered early XC60 D5 models, prized for torque and refinement but requiring timely timing belt replacement to avoid catastrophic failure.
commonFailurePoints: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC60 2.4L diesel engines

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L Drive-E petrol family powers T5 and T6 XC60 variants, offering strong performance with reasonable economy, though timing chain and supercharger concerns require attention on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC60 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The rare 3.0L turbocharged inline-six powered early XC60 T6 AWD models, delivering flagship performance but with limited UK availability and higher replacement costs due to complexity.
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC60 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 3.2L SI6 inline-six offered smooth, reliable power for early XC60 models, though fuel economy lags behind newer turbo units and oil sludge can develop with extended service intervals.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain guide wear on high mileage
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 3.2L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Volvo XC60 Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The T8 Twin Engine combines a 2.0L Drive-E petrol with an electric rear axle for performance and efficiency, though ERAD bearing failures require specialist attention on higher-mileage examples.
items.cta: Enquire about Volvo XC60 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Volvo XC60 2.0L Mild Hybrid Engines
items.description: The B4/B5/B6 mild-hybrid family adds 48V technology to Drive-E petrol and diesel engines, improving efficiency while maintaining performance, though the belt-starter generator requires specialist diagnosis if faulty.
items.cta: Enquire about Volvo XC60 2.0L mild hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.h2: Volvo XC60 Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Volvo XC60 engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early D5 diesels to the latest T8 Recharge hybrids.

items.title: Volvo XC60 Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powers the majority of UK XC60s on the road, with the 2.0L D4 and 2.4L D5 dominating replacement enquiries due to high sales volume and known timing belt/injector failure patterns.
items.descriptor: Diesel powers the majority of UK XC60s on the road, with the 2.0L D4 and 2.4L D5 dominating replacement enquiries due to high sales volume and known timing belt/injector failure patterns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC60 Diesel Engine Families
families: D4204T5 - Most common D4 code (2014-2017); injector seal leaks and timing belt concerns drive replacement demand
D5244T10 - Legendary 2.4L five-cylinder D5; piston ring wear and timing belt failures common on 80k+ mile examples
D4204T14 - Updated D4 with revised rings (2016+); reduced oil consumption but EGR/AdBlue faults appear on high mileage
D4204T23 - B4 mild-hybrid diesel (2020+); 48V system adds complexity but improves efficiency
foundIn: P3 Generation (2008-2017) - D3/D4/D5 variants with 2.0L/2.4L diesels
P5 Generation (2017-present) - D4/B4 variants with 2.0L Drive-E and mild-hybrid technology
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy and torque for towing
Timing belt failure risk on pre-2016 units if not replaced by 108k miles
Injector seal leaks causing diesel odour and rough idle on D4204T5
High UK replacement demand on D4/D5 variants due to volume and failure patterns
items.cta: Get quotes for Volvo XC60 Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Volvo XC60 Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol XC60 variants grew significantly from 2017 with the Drive-E family, offering strong performance but introducing timing chain and supercharger concerns on higher-mileage examples.
items.descriptor: Petrol XC60 variants grew significantly from 2017 with the Drive-E family, offering strong performance but introducing timing chain and supercharger concerns on higher-mileage examples.
items.familiesLabel: Common Volvo XC60 Petrol Engine Families
families: B4204T19 - T5 Drive-E petrol (2017-2021); timing chain tensioner wear and carbon buildup common failure points
B4204T29 - T6 twin-charged (2017-2021); supercharger clutch failure and timing chain concerns drive replacement enquiries
B6324S - 3.2L SI6 naturally aspirated (2009-2014); reliable but thirsty, oil sludge risk with extended intervals
B6304T4 - 3.0L turbo inline-six (2010-2016); rare UK-spec performance unit with turbo seal failure risks
foundIn: P3 Generation (2008-2017) - 3.2L SI6 and 3.0L T6 petrol variants
P5 Generation (2017-present) - T5/T6 Drive-E and B5/B6 mild-hybrid petrol variants
knownFor: Strong acceleration and refinement versus diesel counterparts
Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold-start rattles on early Drive-E units
Supercharger clutch failure on T6 variants leading to loss of low-end boost
Carbon buildup on intake valves requiring walnut blasting on direct-injection units
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 Petrol engine prices

items.title: Volvo XC60 Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The T8 Twin Engine and Recharge plug-in hybrids combine petrol performance with electric efficiency, though ERAD failures and battery degradation require specialist replacement expertise.
items.descriptor: The T8 Twin Engine and Recharge plug-in hybrids combine petrol performance with electric efficiency, though ERAD failures and battery degradation require specialist replacement expertise.
families: B4204T48 - T8 Twin Engine (2018-2021); ERAD bearing failures common on 40k-80k mile examples
B4204TSH - Updated T8 Recharge (2021+); larger battery and improved thermal management
2.0L turbo petrol drives front wheels; electric motor (ERAD) drives rear axle for AWD and EV-only mode
Regenerative braking recharges high-voltage battery; petrol engine engages for longer trips or high demand
Replacement requires hybrid-certified technicians and specialist diagnostic equipment for high-voltage systems
importantNotes: ERAD (Electric Rear Axle Drive) bearing failure is the most common hybrid-specific failure point
High-voltage battery degradation reduces EV-only range after 70k miles on early T8 units
Specialist hybrid-trained fitters required; not all engine suppliers can handle T8 replacements
items.cta: Enquire about Volvo XC60 T8 hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: Volvo XC60 Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Volvo XC60's production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: P3 Launch: Five-Cylinder Diesels and Six-Cylinder Petrols
years.description: The first-generation XC60 launched with Volvo's proven five-cylinder diesels and naturally aspirated/turbo six-cylinder petrols, establishing the model's premium SUV credentials.
keyChanges: XC60 debuts on Volvo P3 platform shared with S80/V70
Initial engines: D5 2.4L diesel, 3.2L SI6 petrol, later 3.0L T6 turbo petrol added
AWD standard on higher-spec variants; FWD available on base D5 in some markets
knownFor: Robust five-cylinder diesel character with strong torque for towing
3.2L SI6 praised for smoothness but criticised for fuel consumption
Early T6 3.0L turbo delivers strong performance but rare in UK market
Timing belt replacement critical on D5 units by 108k miles to avoid catastrophic failure
years.cta: Get quotes for 2008-2010 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC60 D5 timing belt replacement • XC60 3.2 engine oil sludge • XC60 T6 3.0 turbo failure

years.preview: P3 Facelift: D4 Introduction and Drive-E Petrol Arrival
years.description: The mid-life facelift introduced the efficient 2.0L D4 diesel and first Drive-E petrol engines, beginning Volvo's transition to four-cylinder powertrains.
keyChanges: D4 2.0L twin-turbo diesel (D4204T5) introduced, replacing some D5 applications
First Drive-E petrol engines appear: T5 2.0L turbo (B4204T7) in select markets
Updated infotainment and safety tech; R-Design trim expanded
mainEngines: B4204T7 - 2.0L petrol (240 hp) (T5, limited UK availability)
knownFor: D4 offers strong economy but early injector seal leaks emerge as common failure
D5 piston ring wear begins appearing on 80k+ mile examples
T6 3.0L turbo remains performance flagship but high replacement cost
Timing belt interval remains critical maintenance item for all diesel variants
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC60 D4 injector leak • XC60 D5 oil consumption • XC60 T6 supercharger issues

years.preview: P3 Final Years: Drive-E Dominance and Euro 6 Compliance
years.description: Final P3 XC60 models feature widespread Drive-E adoption, Euro 6 emissions compliance, and the first D4/D5 units with revised piston rings to address oil consumption.
keyChanges: D4204T14 replaces D4204T5 with revised piston rings to reduce oil consumption
Euro 6 emissions standards drive AdBlue/SCR system adoption on diesels
Final P3 production ends 2017; P5 generation launches with all-new platform
mainEngines: D5244T11 - 2.4L diesel (215 hp) (D5 AWD late P3)
B4204T9 - 2.0L twin-charged petrol (306 hp) (T6 late P3)
knownFor: Revised D4 piston rings reduce but don't eliminate oil consumption concerns
AdBlue system faults emerge as new failure point on Euro 6 D4/D5 units
T6 twin-charged complexity introduces supercharger clutch failure risk
High replacement demand as first-generation XC60s reach 100k+ miles
years.cta: Get quotes for 2015-2017 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC60 D4 AdBlue fault • XC60 D5 piston rings • XC60 T6 supercharger clutch

years.preview: P5 Launch: All Drive-E, T8 Hybrid Introduction
years.description: The all-new SPA-platform XC60 launches with exclusively four-cylinder Drive-E engines and the first T8 Twin Engine plug-in hybrid, marking a technological leap.
keyChanges: New SPA platform with improved safety, interior and infotainment
All engines now Drive-E four-cylinder: D4, T5, T6, plus new T8 PHEV
T8 combines 2.0L petrol with electric rear axle (ERAD) for AWD and EV mode
mainEngines: B4204T48 - 2.0L PHEV (303-406 hp combined) (T8 Twin Engine)
popularModels: Volvo XC60 T8 Twin Engine Plus/Ultimate
knownFor: D4 continues injector seal and timing chain concerns on higher mileage
T5/T6 timing chain tensioner wear emerges as common failure pattern
T8 ERAD bearing failures begin appearing on 40k-80k mile examples
Premium pricing for T8 replacements reflects hybrid system complexity
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2020 Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC60 T5 timing chain rattle • XC60 T6 supercharger failure • XC60 T8 ERAD noise

years.preview: Present - P5 Mild Hybrid and Recharge Updates
years.description: Latest XC60 models add mild-hybrid technology to B4/B5/B6 variants and update T8 Recharge with larger battery and improved thermal management.
keyChanges: Mild-hybrid 48V system added to B4 diesel and B5/B6 petrol variants
T8 Recharge receives larger battery (18.8kWh) for extended EV range
Polestar Engineered performance variant introduced for T8
popularModels: Volvo XC60 T8 Recharge Ultimate/Polestar Engineered
knownFor: Mild-hybrid belt-starter generator failures emerge as new failure point
T8 Recharge ERAD inverter faults reported on early 2021 units
48V battery degradation reduces fuel economy gains after 60k miles
Specialist hybrid expertise essential for T8 and mild-hybrid replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2021-present Volvo XC60 replacement engines
years.ticker: XC60 B4 48V fault • XC60 B5 belt-starter failure • XC60 T8 Recharge ERAD inverter

faq.h2: Volvo XC60 Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Volvo XC60?
items.answer: Yes, if your XC60 is in good structural condition with reasonable mileage. A rebuilt engine with upgraded components and a 12-month warranty often costs less than a main-dealer replacement and extends the vehicle's life significantly.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines cost £2,200-£7,500 depending on variant and engine code
Upgraded internals address known failure points like D5 piston rings or T6 supercharger clutches
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides peace of mind versus used engine risks
Consider vehicle value, mileage, and service history when deciding
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Volvo XC60 engine prices to assess value for your variant

items.question: How much does a Volvo XC60 engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £2,200 for a D4 diesel to £7,500 for a T8 Twin Engine hybrid, supply-only; supply & fit adds £800-£1,800 depending on workshop location.
keyPoints: D4/D5 diesel rebuilt: £2,200-£3,800 supply only
T5/T6 petrol rebuilt: £2,800-£4,800 supply only
T8 hybrid rebuilt: £4,000-£7,500 supply only (specialist handling required)
Prices vary by engine code, mileage, and supplier network location
items.cta: Get tailored Volvo XC60 engine quotes based on your registration

items.question: What is the most reliable Volvo XC60 engine?
items.answer: The 3.2L SI6 petrol (B6324S) is generally considered the most reliable XC60 engine, though it's thirstier than newer units; among diesels, post-2016 D4204T14 units with revised piston rings show improved longevity.
keyPoints: 3.2L SI6 (2009-2014) praised for simplicity and robustness despite higher fuel use
D4204T14 (2016+) D4 diesel has revised rings reducing oil consumption versus earlier D4204T5
T5 Drive-E petrol (B4204T19) is reliable if timing chain maintenance is observed
T8 hybrids require specialist care; ERAD failures are common but not engine-block related
items.cta: Compare prices for reliable Volvo XC60 engine options like the 3.2L SI6 or revised D4

items.question: How long does a Volvo XC60 engine replacement take?
items.answer: Most Volvo XC60 engine replacements take 3-5 working days from engine arrival to road test, though hybrid T8 units may require 5-7 days due to high-voltage system calibration.
keyPoints: Standard D4/D5 diesel or T5/T6 petrol: 3-4 days typical
T8 Twin Engine hybrid: 5-7 days for high-voltage system safety checks
Supply & fit services often include vehicle collection and return
Delays can occur if additional components (turbo, injectors) require replacement
items.cta: Find Volvo XC60 engine supply & fit specialists with confirmed turnaround times

items.question: Does a replacement XC60 engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt or reconditioned Volvo XC60 engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty; terms vary by supplier so always confirm coverage details.
keyPoints: Standard warranty: 12 months unlimited mileage on rebuilt/recon units
Used engines typically carry shorter 3-6 month warranties
Warranty covers internal components; labour may be separate unless supply & fit
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity post-installation
items.cta: Compare Volvo XC60 engine warranty terms across our specialist network

items.question: Can I fit a used engine in my Volvo XC60?
items.answer: Yes, used engines are available for common XC60 codes like D4204T5 and D5244T10, but always request compression test results and service history to avoid inheriting hidden faults.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £1,200-£2,800 but carry higher risk versus rebuilt units
Verify mileage, service history, and absence of oil consumption before purchase
Compression and leak-down tests essential to confirm cylinder health
Shorter warranty (3-6 months typical) versus 12 months on rebuilt units
items.cta: Check used Volvo XC60 engine availability with verified compression reports

items.question: What causes Volvo XC60 D5 engine oil consumption?
items.answer: Excessive oil consumption in XC60 D5 engines (D5244T10/T11) is primarily caused by piston ring wear or sticking, allowing oil to pass into the combustion chamber and burn.
keyPoints: Piston ring wear typically appears after 80k-120k miles on high-mileage D5s
Burning oil fouls DPFs and accelerates turbocharger wear if unchecked
Revised rings on later D4204T14 units reduced but didn't eliminate the issue
Rebuilt engines with upgraded rings offer the most secure long-term solution
items.cta: Compare rebuilt D5244T10 engine prices to address oil consumption permanently

items.question: Are XC60 D4 engines reliable?
items.answer: XC60 D4 engines (D4204T5/T14) are generally reliable with proper maintenance, but injector seal leaks and timing belt wear on pre-2016 units require proactive attention to avoid costly failures.
keyPoints: Injector seal leaks common on D4204T5 (2014-2016) causing diesel odour and rough idle
Timing belt replacement critical by 108k miles or 10 years to prevent catastrophic failure
D4204T14 (2016+) has revised piston rings reducing oil consumption concerns
Regular servicing with correct oil specification extends D4 engine life significantly
items.cta: Get quotes for D4204T5/D4204T14 preventative maintenance or replacement

items.question: Where can I get a Volvo XC60 engine fitted near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket's network includes over 100 vetted UK Volvo specialists offering supply & fit services nationwide; enter your registration to find local fitters with confirmed availability and pricing.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £800-£1,800 to engine cost depending on region and variant
Mobile fitting available for D4/D5 units in many UK regions
Hybrid T8 replacements require specialist high-voltage certified workshops
All network fitters are rated and reviewed by real Volvo XC60 owners
items.cta: Find Volvo XC60 engine supply & fit specialists near your location

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned and used XC60 engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned XC60 engines are professionally rebuilt with new wear parts and tested before dispatch, while used engines are donor units from accident vehicles with variable history and shorter warranties.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: stripped, machined, new rings/bearings/seals; 12-month warranty
Used: low-mileage donor unit; requires compression testing; 3-6 month warranty typical
Reconditioned costs more (£2,200-£7,500) but offers predictable reliability
Used engines cheaper (£1,200-£2,800) but carry higher risk of hidden faults
items.cta: Compare reconditioned versus used Volvo XC60 engine options with warranty details
